title: Volume 29, Number 1278, Saturday · 1819-05-15 · arrested night
tagline: A clipping from Volume 29, Number 1278, Saturday (1819-05-15) — ink faded, story not.

text.raw_ocr: THE BENGAL HURKARU érs not exceeding fifiy te march out this morning having de lined to grant them any otuer terms The remainder ofthe gar tisonmade their escape dnring the night The whole were Sutd to have amounted to 1200 men entering the Sawaut Warree country fhe heavy Ordnance and Stores required for the siege of Newtee having to proceed by sca I directed Lieutenant Colonel inlach to march along tho Coas I shall march tomorrow towards Watrree in order to accele rate the lreaty with that Government which will I trast be speedily brought to a favorable conclusion I have the honor to be c o o Signed W G KEIR Major General Camp Rarree 4th Feby 1819 Trae Copy Signed D LEIGHTON ddj Gen of che Army Camp before Fort Rarree 13th Feb 1819 To Mujor General Sir W G Kerr Se c e Str 1 have the honor to report to you the result of the attack on the Enemys works this day consisting of the Ist and ad Brigades which you did me the honor to place under my command with his corps the 2d fattalion of the Qd Regiment aad som field pieces to protect
